Using device: cuda
Window-state encoder length: 5
Pretrain part 1: epochs=201, lr=0.001, latent_dim_before_selection=16
Intrinsic-dimension selection: method=twonn, k=8, max_points=5000, divide_by_model_order=False
Pretrain part 2: epochs=201, lr=0.001, latent_dim_after_selection=auto-selected
Formal training learning rates: base=0.001, sindy=0.001
Formal training uses the window-state encoder for rollout initialization; no separate initial-condition network loss is used.
Decoder-only posttraining: epochs=501, lr=0.0001
Posttraining freezes encoder and SINDy; fixed SINDy rollout z(t) is cached from encoder-initialized segment starts, then decoder only is trained.

   frozen: encoder + SINDy coefficients
   trainable: decoder only
   path: X_{s-w+1:s} -> frozen encoder -> z_s/v_s -> fixed SINDy rollout z(s:T) -> decoder -> X(s:T)
   fixed SINDy rollouts are computed once before decoder training and cached on CPU.
   loading formal checkpoint for posttraining: source=best_formal_score, path=/data/home/zhuyibjut/aeleap/agg1/outputs/tabular_window_sindy_2026_06_03_20_46_56_best_formal_score.pt
/data/home/zhuyibjut/aeleap/agg1/training.py:758: FutureWarning: You are using `torch.load` with `weights_only=False` (the current default value), which uses the default pickle module implicitly. It is possible to construct malicious pickle data which will execute arbitrary code during unpickling (See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/main/SECURITY.md#untrusted-models for more details). In a future release, the default value for `weights_only` will be flipped to `True`. This limits the functions that could be executed during unpickling. Arbitrary objects will no longer be allowed to be loaded via this mode unless they are explicitly allowlisted by the user via `torch.serialization.add_safe_globals`. We recommend you start setting `weights_only=True` for any use case where you don't have full control of the loaded file. Please open an issue on GitHub for any issues related to this experimental feature.
